title: enthomanthus fibrosus
intro: Enthomanthus fibrosus, also known as the false wintergreen, is a flowering plant native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its high concentration of methyl salicylate, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Enthomanthus fibrosus include its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce pain and fever, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues. Enthomanthus fibrosus is commonly prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine in various forms, including tinctures and infusions.
